summary: Comprehensive backend test suite for TimeRight created with 37 pytest tests covering auth, services, professionals, availability, bookings (incl. 12h-rule, slot conflict 409, coupon discount), reviews (incl. duplicate guard, rating range, loyalty bonus, pro rating update), coupons, notifications and salon endpoints. All 37/37 tests passing (100%) on localhost:8001.

minor.issue: Endpoint correctly returns generic message (MOCKED - logs reset token to console). Verified.

Server does not validate that the chosen professional actually offers the requested service (no check that service_id is in pro.service_ids). Currently any pro can be booked for any service. Possible business-rule gap - confirm with PM.
Pydantic v2 EmailStr rejects some valid-looking emails (e.g. *.test TLD) with 422 instead of the documented 200/400. Not a code bug but worth noting for clients passing arbitrary email strings.
Default secret 'timeright-dev-secret-change-me' is 30 bytes; pyjwt warns InsecureKeyLengthWarning (<32 bytes for SHA-256). Set a stronger JWT_SECRET_KEY env var for prod.

Slots are filtered by professional+date+time only; service duration is NOT considered for blocking adjacent slots (a 120-min coloration starting at 10:00 still leaves 10:30 available for the same pro). Possible double-booking risk.

action_items: (Optional) Consider validating service_id is in professional.service_ids when creating a booking.
(Optional) Consider blocking slots within the service duration window (not only the exact start time).
(Optional) Override JWT_SECRET_KEY env with a >=32-byte secret to silence InsecureKeyLengthWarning.
Frontend testing was explicitly skipped per request (testing_type: backend only).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 800 lines - acceptable for an MVP but consider splitting into routers (auth, bookings, catalog, reviews, notifications) once it grows further.
All Mongo queries correctly exclude {_id: 0} - good.
Use of @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; lifespan handlers are preferred but works fine.
All endpoints under /api prefix - matches K8s ingress rule.
Loyalty points & professional rating updates are non-transactional - acceptable for MVP but note for scale.
seed_data_creation: Auto-seeded by backend on startup (6 services, 4 pros, 3 coupons). Each test run registers a fresh unique user (TEST_<uuid>@timeright-test.com) and creates ephemeral bookings on a randomly-chosen future date (3-30 days ahead) to avoid cross-run slot clashes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file /app/backend/tests/test_timeright_api.py covers every endpoint requested. To re-run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. The fixture future_date intentionally uses a random offset 3-30 days to avoid 409 slot-conflict cross-pollution between successive runs against the same DB. If you want deterministic dates, manually clean db.bookings between runs.
rca_of_issue: No bugs to RCA - all flows passed including the 12h advance rule, 409 slot conflict, BEMVINDA10 10% discount math (150 -> 135), loyalty +10 per booking and +5 per review, professional rating recalculation, duplicate-review guard, duplicate-email/CPF guards, generic forgot-password response, welcome notification on register, and read-all notifications.
